title: Nuclear RNA purification
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2snhl","text":"Protocol for purifying nuclear RNA for qRT-PCR or next generation sequencing analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Split the cells to obtain a ~80% confluent 10 cm dish on Day 2.

RNA extraction Wash the cells twice with ice-cold PBS. Scrap the cells in 1.2 ml of ice-cold PBS. Centrifuge at 1,000 rpm for 5 minutes at 4°C. Resuspend the pellet with slow pipetting in 1 ml of Lysis Buffer B (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8, 140 mM NaCl, 1.5 mM MgCl2, 0.5 % NP-40). Centrifuge at 1,000 g for 3 minutes at 4°C. Resuspend the pellet with slow pipetting in 1 ml of Lysis Buffer B and transfer to an ice-cold 14 ml round-bottom tube. Add drop by drop 100 μl of the Detergent Stock Solution (3.3 % (w/v) sodium deoxycholate, 6.6 % (v/v) Tween 40) under slow vortexing. Transfer to a fresh ice-cold 1.5 ml tube. Centrifuge at 1,000 g for 3 minutes at 4°C. Resuspend the pellet with slow pipetting in 1 ml of Lysis Buffer B. Centrifuge at 1,000 g for 3 minutes at 4°C. Resuspend the pellet in 1 ml of TRIzol using a 21-gauge syringe and incubated 5 minutes at room temperature. Add 200 μl of chloroform and vortex the sample vigorously for 15 seconds. Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C. Transfer the aqueous fraction to a new tube containing 580 μl of isopropanol. Incubate 10 minutes at room temperature. Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 10 minutes at 4°C. Remove most of the supernatant with a 1 ml pipette. Centrifuge at 12,000 g for one minute at 4°C. Remove the remaining liquid with a 10 or 20 μl tip. Resuspend the pellet in 87 μl of RNase-free water, 10 μl of 10 × DNase buffer, 2 μl of DNase I, and 1 μl of RNase OUT. Incubate for 30 minutes at 32°C. Add 100 μl of acid-phenol:chloroform pH 4.2. Vortex 10 seconds, then centrifuge 5 minutes at 13,000 rpm at room temperature. Transfer the upper phase to a new tube and add 100 μl of acid-phenol:chloroform pH 4.2. Vortex 10 seconds, then centrifuge 5 minutes at 13,000 rpm at room temperature. Transfer the upper phase to a new tube and add 250 μl of 100% ethanol, 10 μl of NaOAc, and 1 μl of Glycoblue (or 1 μl of glycogen 10 mg/ml). Invert the tube several times and incubate at -20°C for at least two hours (or overnight). Centrifuge for 20 minutes at 13,000 rpm at 4°C. Remove the supernatant and centrifuge for two minutes at 13,000 rpm at 4°C. Remove the last drops, air dry for 1-2 minutes, and resuspend in 20 ul of RNase-free water. Determine the concentration and the 260/280 and 260/230 ratios using a NanoDrop or another system. Perform the cDNA reaction for qRT-PCR or NGS.
creator: Michael Tellier
